title: CELL STORAGE-02-Freezing and Thawing Protocol for Suspension Cell Lines
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"52r0t","text":"In our institute, cell lines are stored after freezing procedure that provides for a constant temperature lowering by 1 degree/minute. This procedure is useful to minimizes ice crystals formation during freezing process, this allow to reduce the cells damage and, in turn increasing cells viability after thawing. After storage at -80°C or in liquid nitrogen (in relation to subsequent applications or the expected freezing time) cells are thawed rapidly in a water bath at 37°C and the cryopreserving-medium is immediately remove by gentle centrifugation in order to reduce as soon as possible, the DMSO toxic effect.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ada8","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7m18","text":"Currently, in our institute, the freezing/thawing procedure is validated for the following cell lines:","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ueat","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmd56","text":"PC-12, Rat adrenal phaeochromocytoma, small irregularly shaped cells morphology, suspension growth mode.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4at1h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4utrm","text":"After freezing/thawing procedure, cells quality must be assessed by cell morphology evaluation (using Optical Microscopy technique) and time required to reach confluence in the T25 cm2 culture flask, which must not exceed the times indicated in the Table 1. steps: At confluence remove cell suspension from the T25 cm 2 culture flask and recover in 15 mL conical tube.
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 200 x g, °C, Room temperature .
Remove supernatants and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of cryopreserving medium (90% FBS + 10% DMSO).
Immediately aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension into a cryogenic vial.
Place the cryogenic vial(s) in cotton wool, and then place it in a polystyrene box.
Put the polystyrene box in -80°C freezer. (-80°C Freezer, Eppendorf, B U9230-0001)
After a minimum of 24:00:00 transfer the cryogenic vials into a box for storage in -80°C freezer.
Remove cryogenic vial from -80°C and keep it at 37 °C in water bath, this procedure should take approximately 1-2 minutes or until ampoule is slightly thawed.
Resuspend the cells into appropriate cell culture medium ( Table 2 ) and start quality control procedure ( Table 1 ).
Clean the cryogenic vial outside with 70% EtOH, open the cryogenic vial and add 1 mL of prewarmed complete medium for specific cells lines ( Table 2 ).
Transfer the cell suspension into a 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL of prewarmed complete medium for specific cells lines.
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 200 x g, °C, Room temperature .
creator: Farmacologia Medica
